name: Triangle UFOs
description: Triangle UFOs refer to alleged sightings of unidentified flying objects characterized by a triangular shape. These reports gained traction in the late 20th and early 21st centuries, with numerous accounts detailing large, silent, dark, triangular craft often exhibiting unusual flight characteristics. Proponents suggest these objects are either advanced military technology, extraterrestrial vehicles, or a combination thereof. Some theories posit reverse-engineered alien technology being utilized by governments. The most prominent example is the alleged 'TR-3B Black Manta,' a supposed top-secret U.S. Air Force reconnaissance aircraft. Evidence cited by proponents includes eyewitness testimonies, photographs, radar anomalies, and supposed leaked documents. Mainstream perspectives attribute these sightings to misidentification of conventional aircraft (particularly experimental planes like the F-117 Nighthawk or B-2 Spirit), atmospheric phenomena, hoaxes, or psychological factors like pareidolia. Skeptics emphasize the lack of verifiable physical evidence, the inconsistencies in eyewitness accounts, and the possibility of mass hysteria or suggestion influencing perception. Furthermore, the blurry nature of many photographs and videos makes definitive identification impossible. Government investigations, such as Project Blue Book, have historically attributed similar sightings to natural phenomena or conventional aircraft. While the possibility of truly unexplained aerial phenomena remains, the burden of proof rests on demonstrating beyond reasonable doubt the existence of technology or entities beyond current scientific understanding.
subtitle: Alleged triangular-shaped unidentified flying objects

claims.claim: Triangle UFOs are advanced secret military aircraft.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness reports near military bases, similarities to known aircraft designs, purported leaked documents.
claims.criticism: No concrete evidence of such aircraft, misidentification of existing aircraft, leaked documents often lack verification.

claims.evidence: Unusual flight characteristics, reported silence, large size, and lack of conventional propulsion.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness accounts are subjective and unreliable, atmospheric phenomena can mimic unusual flight patterns, lack of physical evidence of alien technology.

claims.claim: Governments are covering up the existence and origin of Triangle UFOs.
claims.evidence: Lack of transparency from governments regarding UFO investigations, alleged suppression of information, witness intimidation.
claims.criticism: Occam's razor suggests simpler explanations like misidentification or natural phenomena, secrecy is common for advanced military technology, no credible evidence of systematic cover-up.

keyFigures.name: Nick Pope
keyFigures.role: Former UK Ministry of Defence UFO investigator

mythologyConnections: Chariots of the Gods (ancient astronaut theory)

timeline.event: Increased reports of Triangle UFO sightings globally.
Belgian UFO wave, including numerous triangle-shaped UFO reports.
Emergence of the TR-3B Black Manta conspiracy theory.

sources.author: David Marler
sources.work: Triangular UFOs: An Estimate of the Situation
sources.title: David Marler
